title: Ensure that Alpha clusters are not used for production  workloads

input_row.title: Ensure that Alpha clusters are not used for production  workloads
input_row.description: Alpha clusters are not covered by an SLA and are not production-ready.
input_row.rationale: Alpha clusters are designed for early adopters to experiment with workloads that take advantage of new features before those features are production-ready. They have all Kubernetes API features enabled, but are not covered by the GKE SLA, do not receive security updates, have node auto-upgrade and node auto-repair disabled, and cannot be upgraded. They are also automatically deleted after 30 days.
input_row.audit: Using Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list 2. If a cluster appears under the 'Kubernetes alpha clusters' heading, it is an Alpha cluster. Using Command Line First define 2 variables for Cluster Name and Zone and then run the command: gcloud container clusters describe $CLUSTER_NAME --zone $COMPUTE_ZONE -- format json | jq '.enableKubernetesAlpha' The output of the above command will return true if it is an Alpha cluster.
input_row.remediation: Alpha features cannot be disabled. To remediate, a new cluster must be created. Using Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/ 2. Click CREATE CLUSTER, and choose "SWITCH TO STANDARD CLUSTER" in the upper right corner of the screen. Internal Only - General 3. Under Features in the the CLUSTER section, "Enable Kubernetes alpha features in this cluster" will not be available by default and to use Kubernetes alpha features in this cluster, first disable release channels. Note: It will only be available if the cluster is created with a Static version for the Control plane version, along with both Automatically upgrade nodes to the next available version and Enable auto-repair being checked under the Node pool details for each node. 4. Configure the other settings as required and click CREATE. Using Command Line: Upon creating a new cluster gcloud container clusters create [CLUSTER_NAME] \ --zone [COMPUTE_ZONE] Do not use the --enable-kubernetes-alpha argument.
input_row.impact: Users and workloads will not be able to take advantage of features included within Alpha clusters.
input_row.default_value: By default, Kubernetes Alpha features are disabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit can be fully performed using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command-line interface. The audit field specifies using `gcloud container clusters describe` to check if Kubernetes alpha features are enabled, confirming that the control is queryable through automated means.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_cluster_alpha_features_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command: 
  ```
  gcloud container clusters describe [CLUSTER_NAME] --zone [COMPUTE_ZONE] --format=json
  ```
- This command fetches the cluster details in JSON format.
- Check the property: `enableKubernetesAlpha`. The expected configuration is `false` to indicate that alpha features are not enabled.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** If `enableKubernetesAlpha` is `false`.
  - **Fail:** If `enableKubernetesAlpha` is `true`.

This automated approach ensures compliance with the CIS benchmark guidance for GKE clusters by confirming production readiness without reliance on alpha features.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The use of the `gcloud` command-line tool to query cluster configurations is appropriate for this control. The `gcloud container clusters describe` command is capable of retrieving the necessary information to determine if alpha features are enabled, making it suitable for automation.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 9
- The automation details are technically accurate. The `gcloud container clusters describe` command is correctly used to fetch cluster details in JSON format, and the check for the `enableKubernetesAlpha` property is valid. The criteria for Pass/Fail are clearly defined.
- The program name `gcp_gke_cluster_alpha_features_disabled` is descriptive and follows a logical naming convention, although it could be slightly more specific by including "production" to emphasize the context of the check.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The program name could be improved to `gcp_gke_cluster_alpha_features_disabled_for_production` to explicitly indicate that the check is for production readiness.
- Consider adding a step to ensure that the command is executed with appropriate permissions, as accessing cluster details requires specific IAM roles (e.g., Viewer or Editor role on the project).
- It might be beneficial to include a note on handling regional clusters, as the command uses `--zone`, which is specific to zonal clusters. For regional clusters, `--region` should be used instead.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-founded, leveraging appropriate GCP capabilities and providing clear criteria for compliance. The use of `gcloud` is well-suited for this automated check.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the program name to `gcp_gke_cluster_alpha_features_disabled_for_production` for greater clarity on the context of the check.
- Ensure documentation includes notes on required IAM permissions and handling both zonal and regional clusters.
